### Fix 1

Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:

  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: link

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

### Fix 2
Line: 79
Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core


### Fix 3
Lines: 86, 95, 105, 115
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ActionGroup

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 86):
   85          {reset && (
   86            <Button
   87              variant="link"

  (line 95):
   94          )}
   95          <Button
   96            type="button"

  (line 105):
  104          {submitDanger ? (
  105            <Button
  106              type="submit"

  (line 115):
  114          ) : (
  115            <Button
  116              type="submit"

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/generic/ModalTitle.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1: `Button.variant` type changed
The variant values `"link"`, `"secondary"`, `"danger"`, `"primary"` are all still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. All current values are still valid — **no code change needed**.

### Fix 2: Modal family migration — `ModalFooter` conformance
The rule says `ModalFooter` must be inside a `<Modal>`. But looking at this file, `ModalFooter` on line 37 is a **locally defined component** (not the PF `ModalFooter`). It's not imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. The usage on line 79 references this local component. The conformance rule about PF's Modal family doesn't apply here — these are custom components named `ModalFooter`, `ModalBody`, `ModalTitle`. **No code change needed**.

### Fix 3: Icon children to `icon` prop in `<Button>`
Looking at the Button usages:
- Line 86-93: Contains text `{resetText || t('Reset')}` — no icon children
- Line 95-103: Contains text `{cancelText || t('Cancel')}` — no icon children  
- Line 105-113: Contains text `{submitText}` — no icon children
- Line 115-123: Contains text `{submitText}` — no icon children

None of these `<Button>` elements contain icon children from `@patternfly/react-icons`. They all contain only text content. **No code change needed**.

All three fixes either don't require changes (the values are still valid) or don't apply to this code (local components, no icon children).

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **No change needed** — all variant values used (`"link"`, `"secondary"`, `"danger"`, `"primary"`) remain valid in the new type union. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`.

2. **Fix 2** (Modal family migration — ModalFooter conformance): **No change needed** — the `ModalFooter` on line 79 references a locally-defined component (line 37), not the PatternFly `ModalFooter`. It is not imported from `@patternfly/react-core`, so the Modal family conformance rule does not apply.

3. **Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop in Button): **No change needed** — none of the four `<Button>` instances contain icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. They all contain only text content (`resetText`, `cancelText`, `submitText`).

### Fix 1: `Button.variant` type changed
The variant values `"link"`, `"secondary"`, `"danger"`, `"primary"` are all still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. All current values are still valid — **no code change needed**.

### Fix 2: Modal family migration — `ModalFooter` conformance
The rule says `ModalFooter` must be inside a `<Modal>`. But looking at this file, `ModalFooter` on line 37 is a **locally defined component** (not the PF `ModalFooter`). It's not imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. The usage on line 79 references this local component. The conformance rule about PF's Modal family doesn't apply here — these are custom components named `ModalFooter`, `ModalBody`, `ModalTitle`. **No code change needed**.

### Fix 3: Icon children to `icon` prop in `<Button>`
Looking at the Button usages:
- Line 86-93: Contains text `{resetText || t('Reset')}` — no icon children

- Line 105-113: Contains text `{submitText}` — no icon children
- Line 115-123: Contains text `{submitText}` — no icon children

None of these `<Button>` elements contain icon children from `@patternfly/react-icons`. They all contain only text content. **No code change needed**.

All three fixes either don't require changes (the values are still valid) or don't apply to this code (local components, no icon children).

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **No change needed** — all variant values used (`"link"`, `"secondary"`, `"danger"`, `"primary"`) remain valid in the new type union. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`.

2. **Fix 2** (Modal family migration — ModalFooter conformance): **No change needed** — the `ModalFooter` on line 79 references a locally-defined component (line 37), not the PatternFly `ModalFooter`. It is not imported from `@patternfly/react-core`, so the Modal family conformance rule does not apply.

3. **Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop in Button): **No change needed** — none of the four `<Button>` instances contain icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. They all contain only text content (`resetText`, `cancelText`, `submitText`).
